scope_note: QA SL(2,Z) Spine cert [294]. Primary sources: Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers Oxford ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5 Ch.X (continued fractions, SL(2,Z), Euclidean algorithm); Stern, M.A. (1858) Ueber eine zahlentheoretische Funktion, J. Reine Angew. Math. 55:193-220 (Stern-Brocot tree); Brocot, A. (1861) Calcul des rouages par approximation, Revue Chronometrique 3:186-194. CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): Define L=[[1,0],[1,1]] ((b,e)->(b,b+e)) and R=[[1,1],[0,1]] ((b,e)->(b+e,e)), both in SL(2,Z) (det=1). The Fibonacci/QA T-operator M=[[0,1],[1,1]] has det(M)=-1 (NOT in SL(2,Z)) but M^2=L*R (in SL(2,Z)). Every primitive pair (b,e) with gcd(b,e)=1, b,e>=1 is reachable from (1,1) by a UNIQUE word W in {L,R}* (free monoid); W is computed by the subtractive Euclidean algorithm on (b,e) and len(W)=number of Euclidean steps. Pell chain words follow period-4 descending pattern (R,L,L,R)^inf: (1,1)='', (3,2)='LR', (7,5)='RLLR', (17,12)='LRRLLR', etc. This is the group-theoretic backbone of the Ford circle arc: all QA orbit structure arises from SL(2,Z) acting on (Z/mZ)^2, with L and R as the two generators. Theorem NT: all matrix entries integer; Euclidean algorithm on integers. Mechanism chain: cert [289] (Koenig Pell Ford, M^2 step); cert [291] (Fibonacci matrix orbit periods, M=[[0,1],[1,1]]); cert [293] (shell structure, L,R generators). Lineage: derived 2026-06-01.
